question: Under the pinned never-idle lane model (discrete wakes H=2,000, backlog b0=6; per wake in pinned order: due replenishment lands (+R, R=4 primary), consumption demand fires w.p. p_c in {0.6, 1.0} — consuming 1 if backlog>0, counting a DRY WAKE if backlog=0; organic arrival w.p. q of batch g per regime A1 steady-small (q=0.30, g uniform {1,2,3}) / A2 bursty-real (q=0.10, g uniform on the observed multiset {2,4,5,11}) / A3 harvest-scarce (q=0.05, g uniform {2,3,4}); signal policy N in {0(off),1,2,3,4,6} — if backlog <= N and no replenishment in flight, emit a signal (one outstanding at a time) scheduling +R at t+L, routing latency L in {1,2,4} wakes; 18 cells = 3 regimes x 2 p_c x 3 L; M=300 seeded replications per (cell,N), random.Random(20260719), pinned loop order; sensitivity legs R in {2,8}, b0 in {3,12}, H=500, and a decision-stability leg M=50 seed 20260720, all reporting-only), what are D = dry wakes / demand wakes and S = signals per 100 wakes per (cell, N) — and with N*(cell) = the smallest N with D(N) <= 0.05 AND S(N) <= 25 and dD(cell) = D(0) - D(N*), does the result land REJECT-a / APPROVE / REJECT-b / NULL per the pre-registered rule, evaluated in that order?

source.proposal_pin: idea-engine main f7906e5 (landed via idea-engine PR #284)
source.idea_entry: ideas/fleet/backlog-low-water-signal-tuning-2026-07-13.md @ idea-engine main f7906e5 (proposal's own blob link @ e7aa1ddb55176560991cee470efffafce9243370)
source.rotation_slot: inbox ORDER 004 rule 3 — FLEET-BACKLOGS rotation slot, restarting the cycle after 017 unrelated / 018 venture; fully hermetic (the PROPOSAL 017/018 precedent): every fixture is a pinned constant committed with the sim, zero repo/network reads in the verdict session
source.harvest_anchor: websites docs/ideas/backlog.md @ 8f97654 (blob e14bb15), bullet quoted verbatim in the idea file; regime A2 anchored to the four measured re-pin intervals — births {2,5,4,11} / consumptions {5,9,4,3}, pins 144dfce -> 8c19e93 -> d862364 -> c81ce76 -> 8f97654

process.wake_order: per wake t = 1..H, in pinned order: (1) any in-flight replenishment due this wake lands (+R); (2) consumption — the wake demands backlog work with probability p_c; if it demands and b > 0, b -= 1; if it demands and b = 0, count a DRY WAKE; (3) organic arrival — with probability q, a batch of size g lands per regime; (4) signal policy — threshold N: if b <= N and no replenishment is in flight, emit a signal (one outstanding at a time — the wake-hygiene rule) and schedule +R to land at wake t + L
process.n_zero: N = 0 means the signal policy is OFF (the no-signal baseline); no signal ever fires
process.end_of_horizon: a signal scheduled to land past wake H simply never lands (counted as a signal; the outstanding flag persists to end of horizon)
process.draw_order: per-wake RNG draw order, pinned: consumption (rng.random() < p_c, drawn every wake), arrival-fire (rng.random() < q, drawn every wake), batch-size (rng.randrange(len(multiset)) into the regime's multiset, drawn ONLY when the arrival fires) — note the draws are policy-independent (no draw depends on b, N, or L), so the stream is identical across N within a leg position
process.loop_order: cells lexicographic (regime A1<A2<A3, then p_c 0.6<1.0, then L 1<2<4), N ascending (0,1,2,3,4,6), replications sequential (0..M-1); ONE random.Random(seed) stream flows through the whole leg in this pinned order
process.metrics: per (cell, N), pooled over the M replications: D = total dry wakes / total demand wakes; S = total signals * 100 / (M*H); mean backlog = sum of end-of-wake backlog / (M*H) (bloat, reporting-only)

A2.name: bursty-real (anchored to the four measured websites re-pin births)

sensitivity_legs.intake_time_decision: the idea file pins M=300 for the decision grid and M=50 for the stability leg but leaves M for the R/b0/H sensitivity legs unpinned; committed HERE before any code ran: sensitivity legs run the full 18-cell x 6-N grid at M=50 (mirroring the stability leg's committed scale, consistent with the idea file's own '<40% on top' feasibility arithmetic), each leg on a fresh random.Random(20260719) stream through the same pinned loop order

stability_leg.requirement: must reproduce the same ruling as the primary leg (done-when)

band_constants.comparison: exact Fraction arithmetic on pooled integer counts (num/den), never floats, in the decision path; share tests as integer cross-multiplication (count * share_den >= cells * share_num)

decision_rule.n_star: per cell, N*(cell) = the smallest N in {1,2,3,4,6} with D(N) <= 0.05 AND S(N) <= 25 (per 100 wakes); it may not exist. dD(cell) = D(0) - D(N*) where N* exists
decision_rule.REJECT-a: 'organic refill suffices — retire the bullet, spend no grammar token' iff D(0) <= 0.05 in >= 80% of the 18 cells; checked FIRST
decision_rule.APPROVE: 'ship the signal; pin the threshold' iff N* exists in >= 80% of cells AND median dD >= 0.10; recommended threshold = grid-median N*; the verdict must state whether the bullet's own '~3' lies within +-1 of it
decision_rule.REJECT-b: 'no robust threshold exists' iff N* exists in < 50% of cells
decision_rule.NULL: otherwise — a legitimate reportable outcome: the verdict names the flip axis (for each swept axis — regime, p_c, L — the N*-exists share and median dD at each of its values; largest spread = the named boundary; expected candidates: routing latency L and the A3 harvest-scarce regime), and the citable finding is the CONDITIONAL rule, not either camp's default
decision_rule.median: median over the cells where the quantity exists; even count -> arithmetic mean of the two middle values (exact Fraction)
decision_rule.grid_median_n_star: median of the per-cell N* values over cells where N* exists (even count -> mean of the two middles, may be non-integer); '~3 within +-1' test: |3 - grid-median N*| <= 1, exact

consequence_preregistered.APPROVE: substrate-kit gets the evidence row for declaring a `backlog:` token in the kit-owned heartbeat grammar with the sim-pinned threshold as planted default; the websites bullet graduates from '~3 by vibe' to the measured N* (relayed via the manager sweep — the lane is PARKED); idea-engine adopts the signal as consumer #1 (notes-line first, per the fold-in-over-declare rule)
consequence_preregistered.REJECT: either arm — the bullet is retired with the quantified reason routed to the manager sweep and NO kit grammar token is spent (undeclared keys measurably leak into `phase` through hand-kept parsers — the avoided line is a real win)
consequence_preregistered.NULL: the named-axis conditional rule is the citable finding and the cheapest live probe is named: idea-engine logs its own per-wake backlog depth + fired rung in heartbeat notes for ~a week (zero new tooling), locating this seat's real cell before any grammar decision

caveats_preregistered.n4_anchor: four observed intervals BRACKET regime A2, they do not fit a distribution; the anchor leg is reporting-only
caveats_preregistered.latency_mapping: L in {1,2,4} wakes spans same-sweep / next-sweep / overnight without asserting wall-clock; the mapping is stated, not measured
caveats_preregistered.single_lane: manager-side multi-lane signal contention is out of scope by design, named as the follow-up

HAND-1.derivation: fully hand-derived before code ran (p_c=1 so every wake demands; q=0 so no arrivals): w1 demand 3->2, b=2<=2 no in-flight -> SIGNAL#1 lands t=3; w2 2->1 (in-flight, no signal); w3 LAND +4 -> 5, demand -> 4; w4 -> 3; w5 -> 2 -> SIGNAL#2 lands t=7; w6 -> 1; w7 LAND -> 5, demand -> 4; w8 -> 3; w9 -> 2 -> SIGNAL#3 lands t=11 > H (never lands); w10 -> 1. Conservation: 3 + 2*4 + 0 - 10 consumed = 1 = final b. End-of-wake backlogs 2,1,4,3,2,1,4,3,2,1 sum 23

HAND-2.derivation: fully hand-derived: w1 demand 1->0, b=0<=1 no in-flight -> SIGNAL#1 lands t=5; w2 demand on b=0 -> DRY (in-flight, no signal); w3 DRY; w4 DRY; w5 LAND +2 -> 2, demand -> 1, b=1<=1 no in-flight -> SIGNAL#2 lands t=9 > H; w6 demand 1->0; w7 DRY; w8 DRY. dry=5, demand=8, D=5/8=0.625; signal gap w1->w5 = 4 = L (the min-spacing bound is tight). Conservation: 1 + 1*2 + 0 - 3 consumed = 0 = final b. End-of-wake backlogs 0,0,0,0,1,0,0,0 sum 1
